recalls.Description: This recall involves the ADIOS! Super Vinegar All Natural Cleaner, 30%. The cleaner's bottles are labeled on the front with: "ADIOS! SUPER VINEGAR ALL NATURAL CLEANER. 30% Concentrate Indoor & Outdoor use". There is a label on the back with: "Say ADIOS to industrial cleaners with the natural power of vinegar" along with safety information and instructions. The product was sold in a 16 oz. white bottle with a blue cap.
recalls.Title: Compare Brands Recalls ADIOS! Super Vinegar All Natural Cleaner Due to Risk of Poisoning and Chemical Burns; Violation of Labeling Requirements under the Federal Hazardous Substances Act; Sold Exclusively on Amazon.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: Compare Brands toll-free at 888-912-4622 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email at support@comparebrands.net, or online at https://comparebrands.net/pages/product-recalls or https://comparebrands.net/ and click "Recall" at the top of the page for more information.

Products.Name: ADIOS! Super Vinegar All Natural Cleaner, 30%

Images.Caption: Recalled ADIOS! Super Vinegar All Natural Cleaner
Recalled ADIOS! Super Vinegar All Natural Cleaner (Label)

Manufacturers.Name: Compare Brands LLC, of Fort Wayne, Indiana

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from March 2023 through May 2024 for about $13.

Hazards.Name: The recalled cleaning product violates the Federal Hazardous Substances Act because it is deceptively labeled as "Safe to Use" and lacks the required cautionary statements for a poison, posing a risk of poisoning and chemical burns to consumers.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled cleaner immediately and contact Compare Brands for information on how to obtain a free corrected label to put on the cleaner bottle. Compare Brands and Amazon are contacting all known purchasers directly.

recalls.Description: This recall involves the nozzles on Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Outdoor Mold & Mildew Stain Remover (48 oz. & 68 oz.) bottles with hose end nozzles. The stain remover is sold in a blue plastic bottle and has UPCs 879288000077 (48 oz.) and 879288000084 (68 oz.) printed on the back of the bottle in the bottom right-hand corner of the label. "Wet & Forget" is written on the hose end nozzle.
recalls.Title: Wet & Forget USA Recalls 2.7 Million Bottles of "Xtreme Reach" Outdoor Mold & Mildew Stain Remover with Hose End Nozzle Due to Risk of Skin and Eye Irritation
recalls.ConsumerContact: Wet & Forget toll-free at 888-359-4623 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, email at nozzle@realtimeresults.net or online at www.recallrtr.com/nozzle and click on "Safety Recalls" at the top of the page for more information.

Products.Name: Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Outdoor Rapid Application Moss, Mold, Mildew & Algae Stain Remover with Hose End Nozzle

Images.Caption: Recalled Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover – 48 oz. Bottle
Recalled Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover – 68 oz. bottle
Recalled hose-end nozzle sold with Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 3,188 reports of dislodgement of the nozzle insert, including 157 reports of exposure to the cleaning solution and 28 reports that include complaints of skin or eye irritation.

Retailers.Name: Ace Hardware, Costco, Home Depot, Lowes, True Value, Walmart, and other home goods stores nationwide and online at www.amazon.com, www.SamsClubs.com, www.walmart.com, and other websites from February 2018 through November 2023 for between $30 and $35.

Importers.Name: Wet & Forget (USA) Inc., of Elgin, Illinois

Hazards.Name: The hose end nozzle clip can dislodge and allow the cleaning solution to spray on users, posing a risk of skin and eye irritation and other injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Outdoor Mold & Mildew Stain Remover with Hose End Nozzle and contact the firm for a free replacement nozzle. Consumers should not resume use of the stain remover until the replacement nozzle has been installed. Once replaced, the original nozzle should be discarded.
